inline_review:
commit 73f821fe1850e1be4a90fac7106a43c3e77152c6
Author: Li RongQing <lirongqing@baidu.com>

mm/vmalloc: use dedicated unbound workqueue for vmap area draining

This commit introduces a dedicated unbound workqueue for vmap area
draining to prevent watchdog warnings on systems with many CPUs. It
adds the WQ_MEM_RECLAIM flag to ensure forward progress under memory
pressure, and sets up a fallback if the workqueue allocation fails.

> diff --git a/mm/vmalloc.c b/mm/vmalloc.c
> index 61caa55a4402..a52ccd4f3b1a 100644
> --- a/mm/vmalloc.c
> +++ b/mm/vmalloc.c

[ ... ]

> @@ -5422,6 +5423,17 @@ vmap_node_shrink_scan(struct shrinker *shrink, struct shrink_control *sc)
>  	return SHRINK_STOP;
>  }
>  
> +void __init vmalloc_init_late(void)
> +{
> +	vmap_drain_wq = alloc_workqueue("vmap_drain",
> +						WQ_UNBOUND | WQ_MEM_RECLAIM, 0);

Does adding WQ_MEM_RECLAIM here create a forward-progress deadlock?

When vmap_drain_wq executes drain_vmap_work, it eventually calls
__purge_vmap_area_lazy(). This function delegates per-node cleanup to the
default global workqueues (like system_wq or system_percpu_wq) via
schedule_work(), and then synchronously waits for them using flush_work().

Because the default system workqueues do not have the WQ_MEM_RECLAIM flag,
flushing them from a WQ_MEM_RECLAIM context triggers a lockdep warning.

Under memory pressure, system_wq might stall while trying to allocate
kworkers. This would permanently block the rescuer thread in flush_work().
Since the vmap_purge_lock is held during this time, any memory allocation
that reaches the vmalloc slow reclaim path will hang trying to acquire
the lock, deadlocking the system.

> +	if (!vmap_drain_wq) {
> +		pr_warn("vmap_drain_wq creation failed, using system_unbound_wq\n");
> +		vmap_drain_wq = system_unbound_wq;
> +	}
> +
> +}

Will this fallback silently drop the intended memory reclaim guarantees?

If alloc_workqueue() fails, vmap_drain_wq is assigned to system_unbound_wq.
Since system_unbound_wq is a standard workqueue without the WQ_MEM_RECLAIM
flag, the system will lose the forward progress guarantees this patch intended
to provide during memory pressure.
output:
{"findings":[{"problem":"The WQ_MEM_RECLAIM flag on vmap_drain_wq introduces a forward-progress deadlock under memory pressure and triggers a lockdep warning.","severity":"High","severity_explanation":"1. The commit creates `vmap_drain_wq` with the `WQ_MEM_RECLAIM` flag to guarantee forward progress under severe memory pressure, allowing a dedicated rescuer thread to execute `drain_vmap_work`.
2. `drain_vmap_work` eventually calls `__purge_vmap_area_lazy()`, holding the `vmap_purge_lock`.
3. On systems with multiple NUMA nodes, `__purge_vmap_area_lazy()` delegates per-node cleanup via `schedule_work_on(i, &vn->purge_work)` or `schedule_work(&vn->purge_work)`. Both of these queue work to the default global workqueues (`system_wq` or `system_percpu_wq`), which do NOT have the `WQ_MEM_RECLAIM` flag.
4. The rescuer thread then synchronously waits for these tasks to finish by calling `flush_work(&vn->purge_work)`.
5. This creates an illegal forward-progress dependency where a `WQ_MEM_RECLAIM` context is blocking on a `!WQ_MEM_RECLAIM` context. Lockdep's `check_flush_dependency()` will immediately trigger a `WARN_ONCE` splat (`workqueue: WQ_MEM_RECLAIM vmap_drain:%ps is flushing !WQ_MEM_RECLAIM...`).
6. Worse, under actual memory pressure, `system_wq` might stall trying to allocate kworkers. The `vn->purge_work` tasks will not execute, and the `vmap_drain_wq` rescuer thread will be permanently blocked in `flush_work()`. Because `vmap_purge_lock` is held the entire time, any memory allocation that reaches the vmalloc slow reclaim path will hang trying to acquire the lock, deadlocking the system."},{"problem":"The fallback path for failed workqueue allocation silently drops the intended WQ_MEM_RECLAIM guarantee.","severity":"Low","severity_explanation":"1. In `vmalloc_init_late()`, if `alloc_workqueue()` fails, `vmap_drain_wq` is assigned to `system_unbound_wq` as a fallback.
2. The commit author explicitly added the `WQ_MEM_RECLAIM` flag to ensure that vmap draining can make forward progress under memory pressure.
3. However, `system_unbound_wq` is a standard workqueue without the `WQ_MEM_RECLAIM` flag, meaning the system will silently lose the memory reclaim guarantees the patch intended to provide if this fallback path is hit."}],"fixes":"","review_inline":"commit 73f821fe1850e1be4a90fac7106a43c3e77152c6
